description_long: This is a fixed dining table unit with four attached seats, commonly found in fast food restaurants, cafeterias, or public food courts. The table consists of two rectangular surfaces joined together, each with a distinctive color banding in blue, purple, and white. The seats are integrated into the frame, featuring teal cushions and white, geometric, open-back metal supports. The entire structure is supported by a central pedestal and additional metal framing that connects the seats and table for stability. The design is utilitarian, prioritizing durability and ease of cleaning, with no visible decorative embellishments. The materials appear to be powder-coated metal for the frame, laminate or plastic for the tabletop, and vinyl or plastic for the seat cushions.
description: A cafeteria-style table with two rectangular surfaces and four attached teal-cushioned seats, supported by a white metal frame. The table features blue, purple, and white color bands and is designed for public or commercial indoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two joined tabletops with colored bands and the open-back design of the two front seats.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the teal seat cushions and the way the seats are cantilevered from the central frame.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the colored bands on the tabletops and the backrests of the rear seats visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the seat arrangement and the color banding on the tabletop.
attribute_number_of_legs: central pedestal with four seat supports
